perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of two (2) years ("Initial Term") unless terminated earlier in accordance with Section 11 of this Agreement. Upon expiration of the Initial Term, unless written notice to the contrary is provided by one party to the other party at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then-current term, this Agreement shall be renewed for additional, successive periods of one (1) year each (each a "Renewal" and together with the Initial Term, collectively, the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of two (2) years ("Initial Phase") unless terminated earlier in accordance with Section 11 of this Agreement. Upon expiration of the Initial Phase, unless written notice to the contrary is provided by one party to the other party at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then-current term, this Agreement shall be renewed for additional, successive periods of one (1) year each (each a "Renewal" and together with the Initial Phase, collectively, the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Initial Term' to 'Initial Phase' creates confusion regarding the agreement's duration. The rest of the contract consistently uses 'Term' referring to the combined initial and renewal periods, so by changing 'Initial Term' to 'Initial Phase' can lead to disputes over whether provisions related to the 'Term' also apply to the 'Initial Phase'. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the definition of 'Term' includes 'Initial Phase,' but other sections might imply 'Term' excludes it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon expiration of the Initial Phase, unless written notice to the contrary is provided by one party to the other party at least 30 days prior to the expiration of the then-current term, this Agreement shall be renewed for additional, successive periods of one (1) year each (each a "Renewal" and together with the Initial Phase, collectively, the "Term"). In connection with any Renewal, the parties shall mutually agree upon and set forth in a written amendment signed by the parties (i) any changes to the Services constituting Preferred Services and (ii) any changes to the [***], VS Preferred Pricing, percentage Discount (defined in Section 4 below) or Bookings thresholds for all such Preferred Services.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Initial Term' to 'Initial Phase' creates a misaligned terminology. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly states 'Initial Phase' is collectively part of the 'Term'. However, by changing 'Initial Term' to 'Initial Phase', it introduces ambiguity. A reasonable reader might now question whether all provisions related to the 'Term' now automatically apply to the 'Initial Phase', since that is now a separately named duration. The contradicted text assumes 'Initial Phase' means the same as 'Initial Term' when referring to the 'Term', and now they are different, that creates a contradiction. The definition of misaligned terminology leading to an in-text contradiction applies here.

perturbation.original_text: Where PPD is in a position to make a recommendation to Sponsor with regard to imaging services, and VS is able to provide such imaging services in accordance with Sponsor's specifications, PPD agrees to recommend VS to Sponsor. PPD also agrees to maintain, when present, VS's name and logo on all VS generated deliverables to the Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Where PPD is in a position to make a recommendation to Patron with regard to imaging services, and VS is able to provide such imaging services in accordance with Patron's specifications, PPD agrees to recommend VS to Patron. PPD also agrees to maintain, when present, VS's name and logo on all VS generated deliverables to the Patron.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Sponsor' is consistently used throughout the contract to refer to the client of PPD for whom the services are performed. By changing 'Sponsor' to 'Patron' in this section, it creates confusion as to whether this recommendation obligation applies to all clients (Sponsors) or only to a specific subset referred to as 'Patrons.' This contradiction affects PPD's obligation to recommend VS, creating uncertainty in enforcement. The definition of Sponsor is not clearly defined at any point in the agreement therefore, it can create in-text confusion
perturbation.contradicted_text: VS acknowledges and agrees that where VS's Services relate to a specific Sponsor's clinical research study, all payments under a Work Order are pass through payments from Sponsor and PPD shall not have any payment obligations until such payments are received from Sponsor. PPD shall use all reasonable diligence to obtain timely payment from Sponsor in order to make payment to VS within 45 days following the date of invoice receipt. In furtherance thereof, PPD shall ensure that Sponsor is invoiced no later than ten (10) days following PPD's receipt of VS's invoice, assuming such invoice was provided to PPD by VS in accordance herewith and provided that Sponsor's invoice requirements do not preclude the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Sponsor' with 'Patron,' creating a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which refers specifically to 'Sponsor.' The original text states PPD should recommend VS to the Sponsor, VS receives payments from the Sponsor, and PPD will seek payment from the Sponsor. By changing 'Sponsor' to 'Patron' in the changed text, it is unclear whether the obligation to recommend VS and the payment terms apply only to 'Patrons,' or to all 'Sponsors' as intended by the contradicted section. This creates an in-text contradiction and introduces ambiguity in enforcement of the contract and creates uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party, as defined in 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All VS invoices must reference the Purchase Order Number, Service fees, any PPD pre-approved expenses incurred by VS, the remittance address, and the total amount of compensation owed to VS. These invoices must be sent to the following address: PPD, 929 North Front Street, Wilmington, NC 28401, Attention: Accounts Payable.
perturbation.changed_text: All VS invoices must reference the Billing Account Number, Service fees, any PPD pre-approved expenses incurred by VS, the remittance address, and the total amount of compensation owed to VS. These invoices must be sent to the following address: PPD, 929 North Front Street, Wilmington, NC 28401, Attention: Accounts Payable.
perturbation.explanation: Section 5 explicitly defines the billing account number as 'Purchase Order Number.' Changing this to 'Billing Account Number' in the subsequent instruction about invoices creates a direct contradiction. It's unclear which number VS should use on the invoice, causing confusion and potential payment delays. The use of 'Billing Account Number' goes against the contract's initial definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PPD shall assign a billing account number ("Purchase Order Number") to VS for the Services in each Work Order. All VS invoices must reference the Billing Account Number, Service fees, any PPD pre-approved expenses incurred by VS, the remittance address, and the total amount of compensation owed to VS. These invoices must be sent to the following address: PPD, 929 North Front Street, Wilmington, NC 28401, Attention: Accounts Payable.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that VS invoices must reference the 'Purchase Order Number.' Section 5 explicitly defines the billing account number as 'Purchase Order Number.' The changed text modifies 'Purchase Order Number' to 'Billing Account Number.' This change creates a contradiction because now, the invoice instruction uses 'Billing Account Number' while Section 5 equates it with the Purchase Order Number creating an inconsistency regarding which term should be used.
